title: Demo Configuration
description: A demonstration wizard that covers every field type

steps.title: Language & Technology
steps.description: Select your primary language first—this shapes all subsequent recommendations
steps.hint: This selection is crucial—it personalizes suggestions, locked fields, and presets throughout the wizard. The AI assistant will also tailor its behavior based on your language-specific best practices.

fields.label: Primary Language
fields.description: The main programming language used in this project

fields.label: Frameworks & Libraries
fields.description: Select every framework your project uses. Hold Ctrl / ⌘ to select multiple.

fields.label: Enable Strict Mode
fields.description: Instructs the AI to enforce strict type checking, linting rules, and safe coding patterns

steps.title: Project Setup
steps.hint: This information is used to identify your project inside the generated configuration files. The project name should be URL-safe (no spaces).

fields.label: Project Name
fields.description: A short, URL-safe identifier for your project

fields.label: Project Description
fields.description: A brief summary of what your project does. This will appear as a comment in the config file.

fields.label: Project Type
fields.description: Select the category that best fits your project

options.label: API / Backend
options.description: REST or GraphQL API, microservice, or worker

options.label: Library / SDK
options.description: Reusable package published to a registry

steps.title: AI Assistant Behaviour
steps.description: Configure how the AI should behave in your IDE
steps.hint: These settings are written into the tool-specific config file (e.g. .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, or .github/copilot-instructions.md) to give the AI persistent context about how it should assist you.

fields.label: Response Verbosity
fields.description: How detailed should AI responses be by default?

options.label: Concise
options.description: Short, to-the-point answers with no extra prose

fields.label: Focus Areas
fields.description: Topics the AI should proactively raise in suggestions

options.label: Security
options.description: Flag insecure patterns and suggest OWASP best practices

options.label: Performance
options.description: Recommend efficient algorithms and avoid bottlenecks

options.label: Readability
options.description: Prefer clear, self-documenting code over clever one-liners

options.label: Testing
options.description: Encourage unit tests and edge-case coverage

fields.label: Custom Instructions
fields.description: Any additional instructions you want the AI to follow across your entire project
fields.placeholder: e.g. Always prefer async/await over callbacks. Never use any. Use named exports only…
